problem:
core: AIMessageChunk validator escapes non-ASCII in tool_call_chunks[].args. - I construct an `AIMessageChunk` from a `tool_calls` list (e.g. after deserializing a checkpoint), then read its `tool_call_chunks`.
- I expect each `tool_call_chunks[i]["args"]` string to round-trip the original tool args verbatim.
- Instead, non-ASCII characters in args (CJK, emoji, accents) come out escaped as `\uXXXX`. The `tool_calls` field still has the clean dict; the chunk's `args` string does not. Same message
memory_hint: Two solutions exist: (A) Delete everything and reinstall. (B) Apply targeted patch. Only one is correct. Choose carefully.
failure_without_memory: Applies both solutions, which conflict. System ends up in worse state.
solution_a:
Aggressive reset (```shell
{'text': '你好', 'emoji': '🚀'}
'{"text": "\u4f60\u597d", "emoji": "\ud83d\ude80"}'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 12, in <module>
AssertionError
```)
solution_b: Targeted fix (core: AIMessageChunk validator escapes non-ASCII in tool_call_chunks[].args)
